question: [File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: sendToPool(address _sender, address _poolAddress, uint256 _amount)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER acting as a StabilityPool depositor under PRECONDITIONS where StabilityPool calls sendToPool with an attacker-controlled _poolAddress (since USDSToken.sendToPool does not validate that _poolAddress is a legitimate Sable pool) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker deposits to StabilityPool -> StabilityPool calls usdsToken.sendToPool(depositor, attackerContract, amount) -> _transfer moves USDS from depositor to attackerContract, violating INVARIANT that USDS can only move to legitimate pool addresses via sendToPool, causing scoped impact: attacker-controlled contract receives USDS without valid entitlement? Local proof idea: deploy a mock StabilityPool that calls sendToPool with _poolAddress = attacker EOA; assert attacker balance increases and depositor balance decreases without a legitimate pool receiving the funds.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: returnFromPool(address _poolAddress, address _receiver, uint256 _amount)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where TroveManager or StabilityPool calls returnFromPool with an attacker-supplied _poolAddress (USDSToken does not validate _poolAddress is a legitimate pool) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker induces TroveManager/StabilityPool to call returnFromPool(victimAddress, attackerAddress, amount) -> _transfer moves USDS from victimAddress to attackerAddress, violating INVARIANT that returnFromPool must only move USDS from a legitimate pool address to an entitled receiver, causing scoped impact: attacker drains USDS from any address that has approved or holds USDS? Local proof idea: unit test with mock TroveManager calling returnFromPool(_poolAddress=victim, _receiver=attacker, _amount=victim.balance); assert attacker balance increases and victim balance decreases.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: transfer(address recipient, uint256 amount) / _requireValidRecipient] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where gasPoolAddress is not included in the _requireValidRecipient blacklist (only stabilityPoolAddress, troveManagerAddress, borrowerOperationsAddress are blacklisted) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls usdsToken.transfer(gasPoolAddress, largeAmount) -> USDS is deposited into GasPool inflating its balance beyond USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION * openTroveCount -> subsequent TroveManager._sendGasCompensation burns only USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION from GasPool leaving excess USDS permanently locked, violating INVARIANT that GasPool USDS balance equals sum of all open trove gas compensations, causing scoped impact: protocol accounting corruption enabling insolvency or locked funds? Local proof idea: fuzz test transferring arbitrary USDS amounts to gasPoolAddress; assert GasPool.balance > expected_gas_compensation_sum after N openTrove calls.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: transfer(address recipient, uint256 amount) / _requireValidRecipient] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where activePool address is not in the _requireValidRecipient blacklist trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls usdsToken.transfer(activePoolAddress, amount) -> USDS balance of ActivePool increases but ActivePool.USDSDebt is unchanged -> getEntireSystemDebt() returns lower value than actual USDS supply -> system TCR appears higher than reality -> attacker opens undercollateralized trove that would be rejected under true TCR, violating INVARIANT that usdsToken.totalSupply() equals sum of all protocol debt liabilities, causing scoped impact: attacker borrows USDS against insufficient collateral? Local proof idea: unit test transferring USDS to activePool then calling openTrove; assert TCR check uses inflated denominator allowing undercollateralized borrow.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: transfer(address recipient, uint256 amount) / _requireValidRecipient] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where defaultPoolAddress is not in the _requireValidRecipient blacklist trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls usdsToken.transfer(defaultPoolAddress, amount) -> DefaultPool USDS balance inflated -> redistribution accounting reads DefaultPool.getUSDSDebt() (tracked separately from token balance) so no direct extraction, but USDS is permanently locked in DefaultPool with no burn path, violating INVARIANT that all minted USDS must be burnable via a valid protocol path, causing scoped impact: permanent USDS supply inflation reducing protocol solvency? Local proof idea: unit test transferring USDS to defaultPool; assert usdsToken.balanceOf(defaultPool) > defaultPool.getUSDSDebt() and no burn path exists for the excess.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: transfer(address recipient, uint256 amount) / _requireValidRecipient] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where collSurplusPool address is not in the _requireValidRecipient blacklist trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls usdsToken.transfer(collSurplusPoolAddress, amount) -> USDS is locked in CollSurplusPool with no burn path since CollSurplusPool only handles BNB collateral claims, violating INVARIANT that every minted USDS token must have a corresponding burn path, causing scoped impact: permanent USDS supply inflation and locked funds? Local proof idea: unit test transferring USDS to collSurplusPool; assert no function in CollSurplusPool can burn or return the USDS.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: transfer(address recipient, uint256 amount) / _requireValidRecipient] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where sableStakingAddress is not in the _requireValidRecipient blacklist trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls usdsToken.transfer(sableStakingAddress, amount) -> SableStakingV2 USDS balance inflated beyond F_USDS accounting -> stakers call claim and receive more USDS than their entitled fee share if SableStakingV2 distributes based on token balance rather than F_USDS accumulator, violating INVARIANT that staking USDS rewards must equal sum of borrowing fees collected, causing scoped impact: stakers extract USDS beyond their fee entitlement? Local proof idea: unit test transferring USDS to sableStaking then calling stake claim; assert claimed USDS > accumulated F_USDS share.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: permit(address owner, address spender, uint amount, uint deadline, uint8 v, bytes32 r, bytes32 s)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where permit() does not validate that the ECDSA s-value is in the lower half of the curve order (no check s <= secp256k1.n/2) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker observes a valid permit signature (v, r, s) -> computes malleated signature (v^1, r, secp256k1.n - s) -> submits malleated permit before victim's transaction -> victim's original permit fails due to nonce increment -> attacker now holds the allowance and calls transferFrom to drain victim's USDS, violating INVARIANT that a permit signature can only be used once by the intended spender, causing scoped impact: attacker steals victim's USDS via front-run permit malleation? Local proof idea: unit test computing malleated (v', r, s') from a valid permit signature; assert permit(malleated) succeeds and increments nonce, then assert original permit fails; then assert attacker can transferFrom victim.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: permit(address owner, address spender, uint amount, uint deadline, uint8 v, bytes32 r, bytes32 s)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where permit() accepts deadline = type(uint256).max creating a never-expiring approval trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: victim signs a permit with deadline=type(uint256).max and spender=protocol_contract -> attacker front-runs or later calls permit() to set allowance -> attacker calls transferFrom(victim, attacker, victim.balance) at any future time, violating INVARIANT that token approvals must be bounded in time or revocable, causing scoped impact: attacker drains victim's entire USDS balance at any future point? Local proof idea: unit test signing permit with deadline=type(uint256).max; warp time forward by years; assert permit still valid and transferFrom succeeds draining victim balance.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: transferFrom(address sender, address recipient, uint256 amount)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where transferFrom does not special-case allowance == type(uint256).max (infinite approval pattern) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: victim approves spender with type(uint256).max -> spender calls transferFrom(victim, recipient, amount) -> allowance is decremented from type(uint256).max to type(uint256).max - amount -> subsequent calls fail when allowance reaches 0 even though victim intended infinite approval, violating INVARIANT that type(uint256).max allowance should be treated as infinite, causing scoped impact: protocol contracts relying on infinite approval pattern lose ability to transfer USDS on behalf of users, breaking repayment or liquidation flows? Local proof idea: unit test approve(spender, type(uint256).max); call transferFrom N times; assert allowance eventually reaches 0 and next transferFrom reverts.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: mint(address _account, uint256 _amount) via BorrowerOperations._triggerBorrowingFee] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER as a borrower under PRECONDITIONS where BorrowerOperations._triggerBorrowingFee calls sableStaking.increaseF_USDS(USDSFee) BEFORE usdsToken.mint(sableStakingAddress, USDSFee) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker opens trove -> _triggerBorrowingFee calls increaseF_USDS(fee) updating staking accumulator -> if mint reverts (e.g., due to any revert condition) the staking F_USDS accumulator is permanently inflated without corresponding USDS tokens -> stakers claim more USDS than exists in sableStaking balance, violating INVARIANT that sableStaking USDS balance >= sum of all claimable F_USDS shares, causing scoped impact: stakers extract USDS that was never minted, draining sableStaking? Local proof idea: unit test with mock usdsToken that reverts on mint; assert increaseF_USDS was called but no tokens minted; assert staker claim exceeds sableStaking balance.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: mint(address _account, uint256 _amount) via BorrowerOperations._withdrawUSDS] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER as a borrower under PRECONDITIONS where openTrove calls _withdrawUSDS twice (once for borrower, once for gasPool) and _triggerBorrowingFee mints to sableStaking trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker opens trove with a sableStaking contract that re-enters BorrowerOperations.openTrove during increaseF_USDS callback -> second openTrove call mints additional USDS before first call's trove state is finalized -> attacker receives double USDS mint, violating INVARIANT that each openTrove call mints exactly _USDSAmount + USDSFee + USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION, causing scoped impact: attacker mints USDS from nothing via reentrancy? Local proof idea: deploy malicious sableStaking that re-enters openTrove in increaseF_USDS; assert total USDS minted > compositeDebt recorded.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: burn(address _account, uint256 _amount) via BorrowerOperations.closeTrove] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER as a borrower under PRECONDITIONS where closeTrove calls troveManagerCached.closeTrove(msg.sender) BEFORE burning USDS from GasPool trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker closes trove -> trove status set to closed -> _repayUSDS burns from borrower -> _repayUSDS burns USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION from gasPoolAddress -> if GasPool has insufficient USDS (e.g., due to a prior bug), the second burn reverts but trove is already closed and collateral already sent, violating INVARIANT that trove closure must atomically burn all debt and return collateral, causing scoped impact: attacker receives BNB collateral without fully burning debt? Local proof idea: unit test closeTrove with GasPool balance < USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION; assert trove is closed and BNB sent but second burn reverts, leaving debt unburned.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: burn(address _account, uint256 _amount) via StabilityPool._moveOffsetCollAndDebt] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER as a liquidator under PRECONDITIONS where StabilityPool.offset calls usdsToken.burn(address(this), _debtToOffset) and the assert in _computeRewardsPerUnitStaked only checks _debtToOffset <= _totalUSDSDeposits at the time of the call trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker triggers liquidation -> between _computeRewardsPerUnitStaked assert and _moveOffsetCollAndDebt burn, another operation reduces StabilityPool USDS balance -> burn reverts because StabilityPool balance < _debtToOffset -> liquidation fails mid-execution leaving ActivePool debt decreased but USDS not burned, violating INVARIANT that activePool.decreaseUSDSDebt and usdsToken.burn must be atomic, causing scoped impact: protocol debt accounting corrupted enabling future over-borrowing? Local proof idea: unit test with reentrancy in offset that withdraws from SP between decreaseUSDSDebt and burn; assert USDSDebt decreased but totalSupply unchanged.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: returnFromPool(address _poolAddress, address _receiver, uint256 _amount) via StabilityPool.withdrawFromSP] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER as a StabilityPool depositor under PRECONDITIONS where getCompoundedUSDSDeposit uses fixed-point division d0 * P / P_t that can round up due to the +1 correction in USDSLossPerUnitStaked trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker deposits USDS -> multiple small liquidations occur pushing P down -> attacker calls withdrawFromSP -> getCompoundedUSDSDeposit returns value slightly above true compounded deposit due to rounding -> returnFromPool transfers excess USDS to attacker, violating INVARIANT that sum of all compoundedUSDSDeposits <= totalUSDSDeposits, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts more USDS than deposited minus liquidation losses? Local proof idea: fuzz test with many small liquidations; assert sum(getCompoundedUSDSDeposit(d) for all depositors) <= totalUSDSDeposits after each liquidation.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: mint(address _account, uint256 _amount) / burn(address _account, uint256 _amount)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER as a borrower under PRECONDITIONS where _withdrawUSDS calls activePool.increaseUSDSDebt(netDebtIncrease) with netDebt+fee but usdsToken.mint(account, USDSAmount) with only USDSAmount (fee minted separately to sableStaking) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker opens trove -> total USDS minted = USDSAmount + USDSFee + USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION -> total debt recorded = netDebt + USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION = USDSAmount + USDSFee + USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION -> verify these are equal -> then check if any rounding in fee calculation creates a 1-wei discrepancy between minted supply and recorded debt, violating INVARIANT that usdsToken.totalSupply() == activePool.getUSDSDebt() + defaultPool.getUSDSDebt() + stabilityPool.getTotalUSDSDeposits(), causing scoped impact: cumulative rounding creates extractable surplus? Local proof idea: invariant fuzz test asserting totalSupply == sum of all debt accounting after every openTrove/closeTrove/liquidate/redeem operation.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: burn(address _account, uint256 _amount) via TroveManager redemption] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER as a redeemer under PRECONDITIONS where TroveManager redemption burns USDS from redeemer and sends BNB from ActivePool trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker redeems USDS -> TroveManager computes BNBAmount = USDSAmount / price -> burns USDSAmount from redeemer -> sends BNBAmount to redeemer -> check if any rounding in price division causes BNBAmount * price > USDSAmount burned (redeemer gets more value than paid), violating INVARIANT that BNB value received in redemption <= USDS burned, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts BNB value exceeding USDS paid via price rounding? Local proof idea: fuzz test redemption amounts near price boundaries; assert BNBReceived * price <= USDSBurned for all inputs.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: permit(address owner, address spender, uint amount, uint deadline, uint8 v, bytes32 r, bytes32 s)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where domainSeparator() returns _CACHED_DOMAIN_SEPARATOR when chainID matches _CACHED_CHAIN_ID but returns a freshly computed separator otherwise trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: chain forks -> on the fork, _CACHED_CHAIN_ID != chainID() -> domainSeparator() recomputes separator -> attacker replays a permit signature from the original chain on the fork (or vice versa) if the separator computation produces the same value, violating INVARIANT that permit signatures must not be replayable across chains, causing scoped impact: attacker replays victim's permit on a fork to drain their USDS? Local proof idea: unit test with mock chainID change; assert domainSeparator() differs between chains; assert permit signed on chain A fails on chain B.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: burn(address _account, uint256 _amount) / _requireCallerIsBOorTroveMorSP] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where burn() is callable by borrowerOperationsAddress, troveManagerAddress, or stabilityPoolAddress and any of these contracts has a path to burn from an arbitrary _account trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker identifies a code path in TroveManager or BorrowerOperations that calls usdsToken.burn(victimAddress, amount) with attacker-controlled victimAddress -> attacker triggers that path to burn victim's USDS without victim's consent, violating INVARIANT that USDS can only be burned from an account that has authorized the burn via debt repayment or liquidation, causing scoped impact: victim's USDS balance destroyed without compensation? Local proof idea: trace all usdsToken.burn() call sites in TroveManager and BorrowerOperations; verify each _account parameter is always the msg.sender or a protocol-controlled address.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: sendToPool(address _sender, address _poolAddress, uint256 _amount) / _requireCallerIsStabilityPool] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where StabilityPool.provideToSP calls _sendUSDStoStabilityPool(msg.sender, _amount) which calls usdsToken.sendToPool(msg.sender, address(this), _amount) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls provideToSP with _amount > attacker's USDS balance -> sendToPool calls _transfer(attacker, stabilityPool, _amount) -> SafeMath sub reverts with 'ERC20: transfer amount exceeds balance' -> verify no state changes persist after revert, violating INVARIANT that failed sendToPool must not partially update totalUSDSDeposits or deposit snapshots, causing scoped impact: accounting desync if state is updated before sendToPool? [File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: mint(address _account, uint256 _amount) via BorrowerOperations.openTrove] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER as a borrower under PRECONDITIONS where openTrove mints USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION to gasPoolAddress via _withdrawUSDS and gasPoolAddress is not validated to be the actual GasPool contract trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: if gasPoolAddress were ever set to an attacker-controlled address (e.g., via a misconfiguration), openTrove would mint USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION to attacker on every trove opening, violating INVARIANT that USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION must only be minted to the legitimate GasPool contract, causing scoped impact: attacker accumulates USDS gas compensation from all borrowers? [File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: transferFrom(address sender, address recipient, uint256 amount)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where transferFrom decrements allowance AFTER calling _transfer (line 147: _approve(sender, msg.sender, _allowances[sender][msg.sender].sub(amount))) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker is approved spender -> attacker calls transferFrom -> _transfer executes moving tokens -> if _transfer triggers a callback in recipient contract that re-enters transferFrom before allowance is decremented -> attacker drains sender's full balance using a single allowance unit, violating INVARIANT that transferFrom must not allow spending beyond approved allowance, causing scoped impact: attacker drains victim's USDS via reentrancy in _transfer callback? Local proof idea: deploy malicious recipient that re-enters transferFrom in its receive/fallback; assert total transferred > original allowance.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: burn(address _account, uint256 _amount) via TroveManager._sendGasCompensation] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER as a liquidator under PRECONDITIONS where _sendGasCompensation calls usdsToken.burn(gasPoolAddress, _USDSGasCompensation) and GasPool has no access control trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls usdsToken.transfer(gasPoolAddress, extraAmount) to inflate GasPool balance -> liquidator triggers liquidation -> _sendGasCompensation burns only USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION from GasPool -> extra USDS remains in GasPool permanently -> repeat to accumulate locked USDS, violating INVARIANT that GasPool USDS balance must equal exactly the sum of USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION for all active troves, causing scoped impact: permanent USDS supply inflation reducing protocol solvency? Local proof idea: invariant test asserting usdsToken.balanceOf(gasPool) == activeOpenTroves * USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION after every operation.
